steps.title: Look for a concrete example
steps.content: The student wants a specific example of onomatopoeia. A useful sentence must name an actual onomatopoeic word and identify the sound it imitates, not just define the term or discuss language differences generally.

steps.title: Separate examples from background
steps.content: A defines the term and gives its Greek roots, so it is background. C explains why languages vary. D says Korean and Russian use different words but names none. The choice that names "tuk-tuk" and connects it to a heartbeat gives a specific example. The answer is B.
